gems.title: Gems
gems.content:
Gems

All gems now have a single quality level. Vanilla D2R gem qualities (e.g. chipped, flawed, normal, flawless, perfect) have been removed from regular drop tables.

Some hardcoded quest rewards (Andariel, Hell Forge, Gem Shrines) will still drop inferior gem types.

Transmute inferior gems with the Gem Bag in the Horadric Cube for credits!

All gems drop from monsters that are level 18 and up (roughly Act 2, Arcane Sanctuary), and require level 18 to use.

Gem Cluster

A new type of gem that is a prismatic combination of various gem colors that can be transmuted together with the Gem Bag for 20-30 gem credits. You can transmute up to 99 at one time though they cannot be transmuted at the same time as normal gems.

It can be obtained at random from Hell Terror Zone monsters or created by recylcling.

See this page for more information on drops.

See this page for more information on recycling.

Gem Storage: The Gem Bag

All new characters start with a Gem Bag in their inventory.

Replacements can be purchased from vendors should you somehow misplace it.

How it works

The Gem Bag is used to store large numbers of gems, and uses a "credit" system. Each gem added to the bag adds one credit.

Credits are universal allowing you to retrieve any of the seven gem types as needed.

The Gem Bag can hold up to 65,000 gems.

Storing Gems

Add the Gem Bag and up to 99 assorted gems to the Horadric Cube and press transmute to add them.

The game will crash if you attempt to add more than 99 gems to the bag at once.

You can add up to 3 inferior gems (chipped, flawed, normal, flawless, perfect) of the same type to the bag at once. Each adds 1 credit.

Retrieving Gems

Gems can be retrieved using ‘grabbers’ (also purchased from vendors).

Transmute the Gem Bag with an appropriate Gem Grabber to retrieve a single gem.

You can also transmute a grabber by itself to cycle through the types.

orbs.title: Power Orbs
orbs.content:
Power Orbs

These are special items that allow various crafting and item manipulation oppurtunities in order to bridge the gap from the single player experience and no trading to the multiplayer experience with trading.

They each have designated drop locations and requirements which are listed under each orb and detailed on this page.

Magic Find does not affect orbs.

Orb of Conversion

An Orb of Conversion is a very rare item that allows players to turn a Rare item into an equivalent Unique item. 

This provides players with a chance to obtain powerful items without having to find them the traditional way.

An Orb of Conversion can drop from any monster in the game, on any difficulty.

An Orb of Conversion drops on the first kill of Diablo in every difficulty.

To use an Orb of Conversion, transmute it with a Rare item in the Horadric Cube.

Does not work on jewels.

Orb of Assemblage

The Orb of Assemblage is a very rare item that allows players to turn a Rare item into an equivalent Set item. 

This provides players with a chance to complete sets much easier without having to find them all the traditional way.

An Orb of Assemblage can drop from any monster in the game, on any difficulty.

An Orb of Assemblage drops from the first kill of Mephisto in every difficulty.

To use an Orb of Assemblage, transmute it with a Rare item in the Horadric Cube.

Be sure to check and make sure a set item exists on the base first. When one does not it defaults to rare/magic as a return and still consumes the orb.

Orb of Infusion

The Orb of Infusion is a relatively common item that allows players to turn a White base item (or Magic Jewelry) into an equivalent Rare item.

An Orb of Infusion can drop from any monster in the game, on any difficulty.

An Orb of Infusion drops from the first kill of Duriel in every difficulty.

To use an Orb of Infusion, transmute it with a White base item (or Magic Jewelry) in the Horadric Cube.

Does not work on jewels.

Orb of Shadows

The Orb of Shadows is an extremely rare item that allows players to force an item to be Ethereal. It changes no other stats.

The item must have durability, and cannot already be Indestructible.

Bows, Crossbows and certain swords cannot be made ethereal as they have no durability. 

An Orb of Shadows only drops from Unique and Super Unique monsters in Hell Terror Zones.

To use an Orb of Shadows, transmute it with an item in the Horadric Cube.

Orb of Socketing

The Orb of Socketing is a useful tool that allows players to add a random number of sockets to an item.

Each item has a max number of sockets, depending on its level.  

Elite Helms [E] of all bases can have a max of 4 sockets in Reimagined.

Check online sources before attempting to transmute for all other bases and socket level requirements

To use an Orb of Socketing, transmute it with an item in the Horadric Cube. You can reroll existing sockets this way.

Orbs replace essence drops from Act bosses and Key Wardens in Nightmare and Hell.

You can also find them from Champion, Unique and Super Unique monsters in Nightmare and Hell Terror Zones.

Orb of Corruption

The Orb of Corruption is a risk/reward item that allows players to potentially add an additional property to an item.

However, there are non-beneficial outcomes as well, so use wisely!

Transmute an Orb of Corruption with a weapon, armor, or shield in the Horadric Cube. 

You must press the transmute button twice to properly apply the corruption.

Once an item has been corrupted you cannot corrupt it again.

See below for a list of possible corruptions.


Where to find Orb of Corruption

An Orb of Corruption will always drop from the first Baal kill in any difficulty.

Corruption Affixes By Gear

There is a 55% chance a corruption is successful , adding a beneficial property to an item.

  • 1% chance best outcome (top row)
  • 5-7% chance for all other outcomes

There is a 45% chance a corruption fails . A failure can be broken down into three outcomes:

  • 25% chance no property is added
  • 10% chance the item is converted to a Rare Item
  • 10% chance the item is converted to a White Item (or Magic for Jewelry)

DO NOT CORRUPT RUNEWORDS - Corrupt the base item first, and then craft your Runeword.

When corrupting set items you must remove all other items from that set from your character first. 

Put related set items in shared stash. Save and Exit the game, join a new game, and then corrupt your intended set item. 

Failing to do so will apply the corruption to the set bonus and will only be effective when set bonuses are active.

potions.title: Potions
potions.content:
Potions

Health and Mana potions have been upgraded to be percent-based if your HP or Mana are high enough. Health regeneration speed has been normalized to be equal to Mana regeneration speed.

Potion Efficacy

Potions now provide either a flat amount of regeneration, or a percentage of the character's max pool, whichever is higher:
 

New Potions

Magic Find, Experience and Sunder potions have been added and have a 30 second duration. 

Potions can be purchased from vendors in all acts.

Potion effects do not stack but drinking another potion does extend the duration by an additional 30 seconds.

Experience 

Magic Find

Sunder

Sunder potions will break immunities similar to a sunder charm. The effects last 30 seconds and you can have multiple potions active. Unlike the sunder charms there are no negative effects that these potions give.

Fire

Cold

Lightning

Poison

Magic

Physical

keys.title: Keys
keys.content:
Pandemonium Keys

Pandemonium Keys form part of the event chain for the ‘Pandemonium Event’.

Pandemonium Keys can be obtained from Key Wardens:

  • The Countess (Act 1, Forgotten Tower) - Key of Terror
  • The Summoner (Act 2, Arcane Sanctuary) - Key of Hate
  • Nihlathak (Act 5, Halls of Vaught) - Key of Destruction

Pandemonium Keys can also be obtained as a random drop from Hell Terror Zone enemies.

See this page for more information.

Key Storage: Keychain

The keychain is used to store PandemoniumKeys.

Acquiring

You can purchase a keychain from any vendor.

How it works

The keychain uses a "credit" system. Each key that is added to the keychain adds one equivalent credit.

Key credits are displayed separately.

Storing Keys

Transmute a Pandemonium key with the keychain in the Horadric Cube to add a key to the Keychain. You can add up to three keys of the same type to the keychain at a time.

The Keychain will then show the available credits for each key type.

Retrieving Keys

Purchase a key grabber from any vendor - there is a key grabber for each Pandemonium Key.

Transmuting the keychain with a grabber extracts a key from the keychain and subtracts a credit from the keychain.

charms.title: Charms
charms.content:
Charms have been reworked entirely in Reimagined so that Small, Large and Grand Charms all fill a niche and provide a variety of beneficial effects.

In addition to Magic Charms, a number of new Unique Charms have been introduced. At later stages players can begin crafting their own Grand Charms to further customise their playstyle.

Furthermore, Melee Characters can obtain a special and upgradeable Small Charm that allows them to deal splash damage!

Charm Weight System

Charms have a ‘weight’ attributed to them which restricts the number you can carry in your inventory.

If you reach charm weight 41 or more, then your maximum mana is set to 0.

You can check weight in the advanced stats panel. This applies to all Charms.

  • Small Charms have a weight of 1
  • Large charms have a weight of 2
  • Grand Charms have a weight of 3

Splash Charm - Collin's Might

The Splash Charm (Collin's Might) is an upgradeable Small Charm obtained by killing Corpsefire in the Den of Evil on Normal difficulty.

This charm is designed for melee builds. It lowers a Character's minimum and maximum damage in exchange for granting each attack an area of effect (AoE).

As the charm is upgraded the amount of AoE damage increases, and the Min/Max Damage penalty decreases.
 

Charm Progression


Charm Upgrade Path

 

Unique Charms

Below is a list of new Unique charms in Reimagined

For a list of the current unique charms see : https://www.d2r-reimagined.com/uniques?search=charm&hideVanilla=false

the table below is out dated and will be updated at some point in the future when ben stops updating things
 

 

Uber Charms

The Uber Charms, Hellfire Torch & Annihilus, have both seen changes in Reimagined.

Both now have upgraded versions that provide more power and utility that players can work towards.

Players can safely carry one Torch and Soulstone charm at a time - trying to do otherwise will see your hero's health set to 1! 


Acquiring a Hellfire Torch

Each Uber Prime Evil drops a Spirit (Mephisto's Animus, Baal's Calamity, Diablo's Ire).

Transmuting these three items together in the Horadric Cube will produce a base level Hellfire Torch.

These Spirits are also used in various Horadric Cube recipes.

Only one Hellfire Torch can be created per game!

Mephisto's Animus - Diablo's Ire - Baal's Calamity

 

Upgrading the Hellfire Torch

Players can incrementallyupgrade their Hellfire Torch by defeating the Uber Prime Evils additional times.

To upgrade the torch transmute it with the Standard of Heroes and an Orb of Corruption.

 


Obsidian Beacon

The Obsidian Beacon is the final form of the Hellfire Torch.

 

Acquiring Annihilus

To obtain the Annihilus charm you must sell a Standard of Heroes (acquired from ubers 100% drop rate) or a “The Stone of Jordan” (level 29 ring with +1all not the +2 all) ring to a vendor.

The next Super Unique monster you encounter in the same game will be transformed into the Diablo Clone.

Killing the Diablo Clone rewards the player with a small charm called Annihilus.

 

Upgrading Annihilus

Players can incrementally upgrade their Annihilus by collecting various reagents and cubing them together with the charm.

 

 

Black Soulstone

The Black Soulstone is the final form of Annihilus

 

Charm Crafting and Upgrading Sunders into a Crafted Charm

Sunder Charms drop from nightmare and hell terror zones. See the drop table page for more info. https://wiki.d2r-reimagined.com/en/Items/LootTable 

To Craft charms or upgrade a sunder into a crafted charm see the recipes below:

https://wiki.d2r-reimagined.com/en/recipes/Crafting#charms

runes.title: Runes
runes.content:
Runes

Runes, like gems and jewels, are items that you can insert into the sockets of weapons and armor to add benefits (a.k.a socketing). These benefits change if the rune is added to armor, shields or weapons. There are 33 runes in total - a list of them can be found at the bottom of this page. The combination of runes socketed in a specific order will result in the creation of a "Runeword", which we'll get into later.

As an added quality of life feature in Reimagined, runes have been changed to drop as 'stacks'. This makes storing lots of them easier.

You can identify a stack by the little green + symbol in the top-left of the item's icon. The number in the top-right indicates the rune's number. 

Rune stacks have tooltips that tell you what you can do with stacks (stacking, unstacking, upgrading and downgrading). In order to use a rune you need to ‘unstack’ it first by transmuting the stack in the Horadric Cube.

How do I stack runes?

As runes drop as stacks they will auto-stack in your inventory. You can also click/drag them on top of each other in your inventory or personal stash. The shared stash is hardcoded to not allow combining of items, so you must first remove items from it to stack them.

If you have an unstacked rune, simply transmute it with a rune stack of the same type in the Horadric Cube to merge them.

How do I unstack runes?

To unstack a rune, put the stack in the Horadric Cube and press transmute.

- Transmuting a stack with a quantity of 1 will convert it to a single, useable rune.

- Transmuting a stack with a quantity greater than 1 will create a single, useable rune and reduce quantity of the stack by 1.

How do I upgrade runes?

Upgrading runes requires 2 runes of the same tier to create 1 rune of the next tier up.

Upgrading can be done in two ways:

1) Transmute two unstacked runes and a Tome of Town Portal in the Horadric Cube.

2) Transmute a stack (quantity must be 2 or more) and a Tome of Town Portal in the Horadric Cube. This will generate a single, unstacked rune of the next tier up, and reduces quantity of the stack by two.

How do I downgrade runes?

Downgrading a rune yields only one rune of a tier lower.

Downgrading can be done in two ways:

1) Transmute an unstacked rune and a Tome of Identification in the Horadric Cube.

2) Transmute a stack (quantity must be 1 or more) and a Tome of Identification in the Horadric Cube. This will generate a single, unstacked rune of the tier lower, and reduces quantity of the stack by one.

Where do runes drop?

Runes can drop from any monster in the game, though some monsters are more likely to drop them than others.

  • Normal difficulty monsters can drop up to a DOL Rune.
  • Nightmare difficulty monsters can drop up to a CHAM Rune.
  • Hell difficulty monsters can drop up to a ZOD Rune.

A Super Unique monster in each Act has been given a Rune drop table akin to the Countess. These monsters are often referred to as ‘Rune Bosses’. You can read more information about these special Rune drop tables and how they are affected by player count on MaxRoll.

These bosses drop 2 runes in Normal, Nightmare, and Hell and Normal Terror Zones. They can drop 3 in Nightmare and Hell Terror Zones.

Below is a summary of the Runes that each Super Unique Rune Boss can drop up to:

  • Act 1 - The Forgotten Tower: The Countess
    • Normal: THUL
    • Nightmare: KO
    • Hell: VEX
  • Act 2 - Halls of the Dead: Bloodwitch the Wild
    • Normal: THUL
    • Nightmare: KO
    • Hell: VEX
  • Act 3 - Ruined Temple: Battlemaiden Sarina
    • Normal: THUL
    • Nightmare: KO
    • Hell: VEX
  • Act 4 - River of Flame: Hephasto
    • Normal: DOL
    • Nightmare: UM
    • Hell: LO
  • Act 5 - Frozen River: Frozenstein
    • Normal: DOL
    • Nightmare: UM
    • Hell: LO

See this page for more information.

What are Runewords?

Socketing runes into a non-magical item (a.k.a white 'base item') in a specific order results in a powerful item known as a Runeword. In addition to granting the benefits of the runes themselves, additional stats and modifiers are added that help empower your character's spells and abilities, or grant you new ones.

Runewords require that runes be added in a specific order, with the exact number of sockets required, into an appropriate base item.

For a list of all the Runewords in D2R-Reimagined, visit the mod website's Runewords tab.

Rune List

loottable.title: Drop Tables
loottable.content:
﻿

Drop Tables

Information regarding various special items and where they have a chance to drop and information on what designation each type of monster is considered when looking for loot.
 

Boss Definitions

Act Bosses

Item in parentheses is the orb the boss drops on the quest completion kill or the first kill in every difficulty.

  • Andariel
  • Duriel (Infusion)
  • Mephisto (Assemblage)
  • Diablo (Conversion)
  • Baal (Corruption)

 

Key Bosses

Item in parentheses is the target-farmable key the boss drops.

Key drop rates are 5% at p1 and 6% at p7

  • Countess (Terror)
  • Summoner (Hate)
  • Nihlathak (Destruction)

 

Rune Bosses

The letter in parentheses corresponds to the bosses rune tier. These bosses do drop more runes on lower player settings.

  • Countess (L)
  • Bloodwitch (L)
  • Battlemaiden (L)
  • Hephasto (H)
  • Frozenstein (H)

 

Lieutenants

These are special monsters that guard various bosses throughout the game. They are listed by name followed by the boss they currently guard.

  • Act 1
    • Raven's Eye and Talon - Blood Raven
    • The Smith's Arsenal - Smith
    • Tormented Sister - Andariel
  • Act 3
    • Council in Durance of Hate Level 3 - Mephisto
  • Act 4
    • Legion and Leviathan - Izual
    • Hephasto's Arsenal & Kudaai - Hephasto
  • Act 5
    • Shenk's Lieutenant - Shenk
    • Baal's Hand (Fighter and Mage) - Baal

 

Special Uniques

These are super uniques with their own loot tables.

  • Blood Raven
  • Griswold
  • Smith
  • Radamant
  • Act 3 Council Members (Gold Name Only)
  • Izual
  • Cow King

 

Super Uniques & Uniques

Super uniques are named bosses that always appear in a certain map and/or location, such as A1 Stony Field's Rakanishu or Act 5 Frigid Highlands Rectifier. They have gold names and a set property or two in addition to random properties.

Uniques are randomly generated leaders of a pack of minions that spawn in an area as you explore it. They have gold names and random properties.

 

Champions

A group of three monsters that spawn in an area as you explore it that have blue names.

 

Drop % of Terror zone specific things


Hell TZ Act Bosses


p1:
Gem cluster = .91%
Socket Orb = .91%
Corruption Orb = .91%
Shadow orb =   .13%
Sunder Charms = .42% (then it picks a random one from the 6)

p7:
Gem cluster = 3.48%
Socket Orb = 3.48%
Corruption Orb = 3.48%
Shadow orb =   .50%
Sunder Charms = 1.68% (then it picks a random one from the 6)
 


Hell TZ everything else with a gold name


p1:
Gem cluster = .91%
Socket Orb = .91%
Corruption Orb = .91%
Pandemonium key = .52%
Shadow orb =   .13%
Sunder Charms = .42% (then it picks a random one from the 6)

p7:
Gem cluster = 3.48%
Socket Orb = 3.48%
Corruption Orb = 3.48%
Pandemonium key = 1.99%
Shadow orb =   .50%
Sunder Charms = 1.68% (then it picks a random one from the 6)

 

Who Can Drop What, Where?

This is a legend for the table below:

  • "S" - Socket Orb
  • "C" - Corruption Orb
  • "E" - Shadow Orb
  • "R" - Gem Cluster
  • "K" - Random Pandemonium Key
  • “I” - Sunder Charms

 

 

Runes

This table is for the rune bosses listed above and the counts are expected rune drops when the /players count is = 1 though there is a slight variance. In addition to these "guaranteed" runes, they can drop any rune up to the area level or TZ area level off of the generic loot tables.
